customManagers.description: Pick up action version pins inside `vars.toml` (template root, e.g. yukimemi/pj-rust) and `.kata/vars.toml` (consumer-side, after kata seeds it from a pj-rust v0.3.1+ stack). Each pin is preceded by a `# renovate: datasource=... depName=...` annotation; the regex captures both plus the value on the next non-comment line.

Pick up `uses:` action references inside `.template` workflow files (kata sources). Renovate's default github-actions manager only scans `.yml`/`.yaml`; the `.template` suffix keeps GitHub Actions from auto-running the file in the template repo itself. This regex lets Renovate bump those `uses:` versions anyway. Fires in template repos (pj-rust-cli `release.yml.template`, pj-firebase `deploy.yml.template`, …) and is a no-op in consumer projects (their `.yml` is rendered, not `.template`).

packageRules.description: Auto-merge minor / patch / pin / digest bumps once CI passes. Major bumps still require human review.
Group all GitHub Actions updates into one PR (covers both the built-in github-actions manager and the regex customManager).

Consumer-side kata-managed workflow files — action bumps happen UPSTREAM in the source pj-* template (pj-base's `kata-apply.yml.tera`, pj-rust's `ci.yml.tera`, pj-rust-cli's `release.yml.template`, pj-firebase's `deploy.yml.template`) and flow to consumers via `kata apply`. Renovate-driven local bumps would either be reverted (`when=always` workflows like ci.yml and kata-apply.yml) or diverge silently (`when=once` workflows like release.yml and deploy.yml). Disable consumer-side scanning to avoid both failure modes. The canonical pins live in `.kata/vars.toml` (customManager scans there) and in the `.template`/`.tera` sources upstream.
